mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS raises expected household income and aggregate demand, leading investors to mark up earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS growth signals produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward instances or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0%, higher short rates raise equity discount rates and increase financing costs, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or a nonnegative mean excess return.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signals inflation-driven tightening, which lowers the present value of future earnings and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL has a nonnegative coefficient for 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward regressions or if triggered returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong short-term growth in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth above 1% is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of triggered cases or if mean triggered returns are below unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving capacity utilization and goods-sector output, which supports operating leverage and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if increases in INDPRO do not predict higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than decreases in INDPRO, or if the walk-forward directional hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses above 1900, residential construction demand signals strong household formation and durable-goods spillovers, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings above 1900 are followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if positive return frequency after triggers is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with a rising FEDFUNDS rate signals late-cycle labor tightness and policy restraint, which pressures margins and valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with tight labor market
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-UNRATE and rising-FEDFUNDS trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or if the interaction coefficient is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real activity and consumer spending momentum, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not generate higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods when either proxy is nonpositive, or if the interaction Rank IC is below 0.03.
